---
title: "Publications"
subtitle: "Publications from Jonas Cremer and the Cremer Lab. For a complete list, see [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2328-5152)."
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv("data/publications.csv", dtype=str).fillna("")

def doi_url(doi):
    """Normalize DOI to a full URL."""
    if not doi:
        return ""
    doi = doi.strip()
    if doi.startswith("http"):
        return doi
    return f"https://doi.org/{doi}"

def render_links(row):
    links = []
    if row["doi"]:
        links.append(f'<a href="{doi_url(row["doi"])}">DOI</a>')
    if row["preprint"]:
        links.append(f'<a href="{doi_url(row["preprint"])}">Preprint</a>')
    if row["github"]:
        links.append(f'<a href="{row["github"]}">Code</a>')
    if row["paper_website"]:
        links.append(f'<a href="{row["paper_website"]}">Project</a>')
    return " ".join(links)

def render_journal_info(row):
    parts = [f"<em>{row['journal']}</em>"]
    if row["volume"]:
        parts.append(row["volume"])
    if row["issue"]:
        parts.append(row["issue"])
    if row["page"]:
        parts.append(f"p.{row['page']}")
    return ", ".join(parts)

# Sort by year descending
df["year"] = df["year"].astype(int)
df = df.sort_values("year", ascending=False)

for year, group in df.groupby("year", sort=False):
    print(f'\n<div class="pub-year-header">{year}</div>\n')
    for _, row in group.iterrows():
        links = render_links(row)
        journal_info = render_journal_info(row)
        note_html = f'<div class="pub-note">{row["notes"]}</div>' if row["notes"] else ""
        print(f"""
<div class="pub-entry">
  <div class="pub-title">{row['title']}</div>
  <div class="pub-authors">{row['authors']}</div>
  <div class="pub-journal">{journal_info}</div>
  <div class="pub-links">{links}</div>
  {note_html}
</div>
""")
```
